title: VIT Config
description: Configuration for VIT (Version It!) — version and release manager.

semantic.description: If true, the changelog is fully regenerated from git tags (semantic mode). If false, it is managed manually.

git.description: Git configuration for commits, releases and branches.

defaultCommitMessage.description: Default commit message for regular commit operations.

releaseCommitMessage.description: Default commit message when performing a release.

changelogCommitMessage.description: Default commit message when updating the changelog.

strict.description: If true, releases are blocked from branches not listed in 'releaseBranches'. If false, only a warning is shown.

releaseBranches.description: List of branches from which releases are allowed. Supports glob patterns (e.g. 'release/*').

items.description: Allowed branch name or glob pattern (e.g. 'main', 'release/*').

preReleaseBranches.description: List of branches from which pre-releases are allowed. When VIT detects the current branch here, it automatically uses a prerelease bump (e.g. 1.2.0-beta.1). Fully opt-in — if omitted, no pre-release behavior is applied.

name.description: Branch name (exact match, e.g. 'beta', 'alpha', 'next'). Does not support glob patterns.

id.description: Pre-release identifier used in the version tag (e.g. 'beta' → '1.2.0-beta.1'). Defaults to the branch name if omitted.

rollbackStrategy.description: 'revert' creates a new commit that undoes changes (history preserved, recommended). 'reset' rewrites history (requires force push, use only on personal repos).

promoteStrategy.description: Strategy used when promoting a prerelease branch to stable. 'merge' performs a local merge into the target branch and syncs both branches. 'pr' bumps the stable version on the prerelease branch, pushes it, and opens a GitHub Pull Request — requires config.github.token (or GITHUB_TOKEN env) and config.github.repo.

github.description: GitHub API settings. Required when git.promoteStrategy is 'pr'.

token.description: GitHub personal access token or GITHUB_TOKEN. The GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable takes precedence over this value.

repo.description: Repository slug in 'owner/repo' format (e.g. 'my-org/my-repo'). Auto-detected from git remote origin when omitted.

envFile.description: Path to a global .env file loaded for all actions. Variables here have lower priority than action-level envFile, env and promptEnv.

projects.description: List of projects managed by VIT. Each project has its own package.json and can be versioned independently.

id.description: Unique project identifier. Used in CLI with --projects and in built-in variables as ${version.<id>} and ${name.<id>}.

label.description: Human-readable project name shown in the interface.

path.description: Relative path to the project directory (where its package.json is located).

tagPrefix.description: Prefix used for git tags (e.g. 'v' generates tags like 'v1.2.3').

types.description: Customize or extend the commit types available in the changelog. Merged with the default types (feat, fix, refactor, perf, revert, docs, style).

label.description: Label shown as the changelog section header (e.g. '🚀 Features').

choiceLabel.description: Label shown in the interactive prompt choice list (e.g. '🚀 feat — New feature').

preActions.description: Actions executed BEFORE the main operation (commit, release or changelog).

postActions.description: Actions executed AFTER the main operation (commit, release or changelog).

onEvents.description: Events on which this action is triggered.

items.description: 'commit' = regular commits. 'release' = version bumps. 'changelog' = changelog-only commits.

promptEnvEntry.description: An environment variable that is asked interactively to the user before the action runs. Has the highest priority over all other env sources.

name.description: Name of the environment variable (e.g. 'SSH_PASS' → available as ${SSH_PASS}).

message.description: Prompt message shown to the user (e.g. 'SSH password:').

validate.description: Built-in validation rule. Currently supported: 'otp' (validates a 6-digit code).

pipelineStep.description: A pipeline step that runs a command and optionally captures its stdout as an environment variable.

label.description: Human-readable step description shown in the spinner.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Its stdout is captured if 'captureAs' is set.

captureAs.description: Name of the environment variable where stdout is stored (e.g. 'VERSION' → available as ${VERSION} in subsequent steps and the main command).

cwd.description: Working directory for this step. Defaults to the parent action's cwd.

continueOnError.description: If true, the pipeline continues even if this step fails.

showOutput.description: If true, the step's stdout is printed to the terminal. Default is false for pipeline steps.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum execution time in milliseconds. null means no limit.

action.description: An action executed before or after the main VIT operation.

label.description: Human-readable action name shown in the spinner.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Can interpolate built-in variables (${version}, ${date}, ${branch}...) and variables captured from the pipeline (${VAR_NAME}).

cwd.description: Working directory for this action. Defaults to the project root. Also determines the value of built-in variables like ${version} and ${name}.

continueOnError.description: If true, VIT continues even if this action fails.

showOutput.description: If true, the command output is printed to the terminal.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum execution time in milliseconds. null means no limit.

envFile.description: Path to a .env file specific to this action. Overrides variables from the global envFile with the same name.

env.description: Static environment variables available to this action. Higher priority than envFile.

promptEnv.description: Variables asked interactively to the user before running the action. Highest priority over all other env sources. Stops headless execution until input is received.

pipeline.description: Steps executed before the main command. Each step can capture its stdout into an environment variable available to subsequent steps and the final command via ${VAR_NAME}.
